Patients can submit details about their medical complaints through the system.
The feature records patient-reported health issues for evaluation.
A user logs their condition information to begin the triage process.
Submitting symptom data initiates the assignment of a priority level.
The registration of clinical indicators allows the system to assess care urgency.
Each case is given a priority rating based on symptom severity.
The severity level determines how quickly the patient should be seen.
Medical staff benefit from automatic prioritization to focus on urgent cases.
Assigning a criticality score ensures the most serious patients are handled first.
The triage category dictates the patient's position in the waiting order.
Patients are arranged in the queue first by priority then by arrival time.
The sorting logic places critical cases before less urgent ones, with earlier arrivals first.
Users experience a fair ordering that respects both medical need and waiting duration.
This ordering produces a sequence where the highest-priority, longest-waiting patient is first.
The waiting list is organized using descending severity and ascending check-in timestamp.
When a patient's priority changes, the queue automatically re-sorts to maintain correct order.
The system dynamically rearranges the waiting list after an urgency update.
Users do not need to manually reorder – the system handles it automatically.
After a severity change, the list is instantly adjusted to reflect the new priority.
The queue self-corrects upon alteration of a patient's criticality level.
The front-most patient (highest priority, earliest arrival) can be fetched and removed.
The system retrieves and deletes the next patient to be served from the waiting list.
Clinicians can quickly access the most urgent patient for consultation.
This operation outputs the next case to handle and clears it from the queue.
The highest-severity, longest-waiting person is dequeued for care.
A live overview shows total waitlist size, sorted patients with priority badges, estimated wait times, and the next case.
The dashboard displays real-time metrics including queue length, urgency labels, and expected delays.
Users benefit from at-a-glance situational awareness of the entire queue.
The outcome is a continuously updated visual summary of waiting room status.
An operational screen presents the current patient lineup with severity markers and wait projections.
